{{Quote|Get me the Plex.|Hazram Namir|<ref name="BTC" />}}
The '''PLX-1''', also known as the '''PLEX rocket launcher''' or simply '''Plex''', was a [[missile launcher]] used during the [[Galactic Civil War]] by the [[Galactic Empire]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]], later the [[New Republic]]. The Plex was designed to bypass [[deflector shield]]s. It was used by [[Rebel Vanguard]]s and [[rocket stormtrooper]]s alike. The Alliance's [[61st Mobile Infantry]] used PLX-1s on the [[planet]] [[Sullust]] in [[3 ABY]] and at the planet [[Troithe]] around [[5 ABY]], where it was feared that Plex armed [[stormtrooper]]s may counter New Republic [[starfighter]]s.

==Description==
The PLX-1,<ref name="BTC" /> also referred to as the PLEX rocket launcher,<ref name="SWC">''[[Star Wars: Commander]]''</ref> or Plex, was a [[missile launcher]]<ref name="BTC" /> designed to bypass [[Deflector shield|shields]] and destroy targets from a distance.<ref name="SWC" /> Considered bulky and cumbersome by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]]<ref name="BTC" /> and [[New Republic]]<ref name="Shadow Fall" /> [[Captain]] [[Hazram Namir]], the launcher possessed control labels and settings could be adjusted to confirm the PLX-1s payload. The launcher was half as tall as a [[human]] [[Gender|male]].<ref name="BTC" />

The PLEX rocket launcher was used by the Rebel Alliance,<ref name="BTC" /> which later became the New Republic, and the [[Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Shadow Fall" /> notably by [[Rebel Vanguard]]s, who were specialized in targeting hidden enemy [[starfighter]] traps with their PLX-1s,<ref name="SWC" /> and [[stormtrooper]]s.<ref name="Shadow Fall" />

==History==
{{Quote|Rebel—New Republic—screw it, rebel ground team is trying to get a bead on them. We've got one Plex missile but we'll do what we can.|A member of the New Republic on Troithe|<ref name="Shadow Fall" />}}
During the [[early rebellion against the Galactic Empire]], [[rocket stormtrooper]]s made use of PLX-1s against the [[Spectres]].<ref name="Recon Missions" /> Multiple PLEX rocket launchers were used by the Alliance's [[61st Mobile Infantry]].<ref name="BTC" /> In [[3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">The sections of ''[[Battlefront: Twilight Company]]'' including the PLX-1 takes place is set twenty-two [[Standard year|years]] after the [[Clone Wars]], which [[Mission to Mustafar|ended]] in [[19 BBY]], according to ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]''. These sections must have therefore occurred in [[3 ABY]].</ref> [[Captain]] [[Hazram Namir]] used one against an Imperial [[airspeeder]] during the [[Siege of Inyusu Tor|capture]] of the [[Inyusu Tor mineral processing facility]] on the planet [[Sullust]] and Plex-armed anti-air gunners were later positioned to fend off more airspeeders during the Empire's counterattack just two [[Standard day|days]] later.<ref name="BTC" />

Around [[5 ABY]],<ref name="5 ABY">{{SW|url=news/shadow-fall-author-interview|text=In ''Shadow Fall'', the Dark Secrets of ''Alphabet Squadron'' Revealed}} establishes that ''[[Shadow Fall: An Alphabet Squadron Novel]]'' takes place approximately half a [[Standard year|year]] after the [[Battle of Endor]], while ''[[Before the Awakening]]'' states that [[Kes Dameron]] and [[Shara Bey]] moved to [[Yavin 4]] around six [[Standard month|months]] after the Battle of Endor. Given that ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates their move to [[5 ABY]], the events of ''Shadow Fall'' must also take place around that [[time]].</ref> Plex missiles were used on the planet [[Troithe]]. It was feared during one scout mission that a Plex-armed stormtrooper may fire upon New Republic starfighters and one New Republic ground team was later limited to having one Plex when fending off [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter]]s of the Empire's [[204th Imperial Fighter Wing]] at Troithe's [[Thannerhouse District]].<ref name="Shadow Fall" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SWC_Vanguard.png|thumb|left|250px|The E-60R was misidentified as the PLX-1 in ''Star Wars: Commander''.]]
The PLX-1 was first mentioned in the [[2014]] [[Disney Interactive]] mobile game ''[[Star Wars: Commander]]'', where it was referred to as a PLEX rocket launcher. The missile launcher served as the weapon of the rebel vanguard, an unlockable breacher unit whose abilities included an area splash damage, firing through shields, and favoring hidden starfighter traps within an enemy base.<ref name="SWC">''[[Star Wars: Commander]]''</ref> The PLX-1 was later identified in the [[2015]] novel ''[[Battlefront: Twilight Company]]'', written be [[Alexander Freed]].<ref name="BTC" /> Freed's [[2020]] novel ''[[Shadow Fall: An Alphabet Squadron Novel]]'' referred to the launcher as the Plex rocket and Plex missile.<ref name="Shadow Fall" /> However, ''Commander'' depicts the PLX-1 in a design lifted from the [[E-60R missile launcher]] from the [[2004]] [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] game ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''.<ref name="SWB">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''</ref> Indeed, while ''Star Wars: Commander'' shows the launcher to stand higher than the carrier,<ref name="SWC" /> ''Battlefront: Twilight Company'' stated that it stood half as tall as a man.<ref name="BTC" /> The [[E-60R]] has since been canonized with the design used initially in ''Commander'' for the PLX-1.<ref name="Legion">{{Legion|set=Clone Wars Core Set|cardname=E-60R B1 Trooper}}</ref> The proper PLX-1 design was used within the 2015 video game ''[[Star Wars Rebels: Recon Missions]]'', which also served as the weapon's real first appearance.<ref name="Recon Missions" />

In Legends continuity, the [[PLX-1/Legends|PLX-1 portable missile launcher]] first appeared in the short story ''A Glimmer of Hope'', written by [[Charlene Newcomb]] and released as part of the [[West End Games]] roleplaying magazine [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]] in [[February]] [[1994]].<ref name="GoH">{{JournalCite|1|A Glimmer of Hope}}</ref>
